College Board AP Course and Exam Description
questions.prompt: A student reviewing AP Research: Research Question needs to show the skill "align evidence to a research question." Which action best fits that skill?

ignoring the source or data in the prompt
Repeat the definition of Research Question only.
questions.explanation: The useful move is to connect evidence to research question; this matches the standards/CED emphasis on reasoning and application.

standardRefs: AP CED: Research Question
College Board AP Course and Exam Description
questions.prompt: Which answer best avoids a common mistake about AP Research: Research Question?

choices: Choose an unrelated fact from another unit.
using a rule from a different unit
Connect Research Question to the prompt context before choosing.
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
questions.explanation: Most missed review questions come from using familiar terms without matching them to the exact context.

College Board AP Course and Exam Description

Specific evidence that proves a claim about source credibility.
A fact from a different course.
describing a topic without explaining why it matters
questions.explanation: The standards across NYS courses and AP CEDs reward evidence used for a claim, not disconnected recall.

questions.explanation: Strong review notes pair the concept with why it matters in the unit.
